---
title: "Predator-prey (consumer-resource) interactions"
author: "Christopher Moore"
date: "Week 05"
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    lib_dir: libs
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, collapse = T)
library(package = "deSolve")
library(package = "phaseR")
library(package = "animation")
```


class: inverse, center, middle

![Grey Grey Owl](https://cdn.thinglink.me/api/image/690547197309091840/1240/10/scaletowidth)

# Predator-prey (consumer-resource) interactions, example I

---
class: inverse, center, middle

![Oak](https://acornsonglendotcom.files.wordpress.com/2011/09/oak.jpg)

---
# Lotka and Volterra
- Alfred J. Lotka, American, first proposed predator-prey model in 1910 but more generally in 1925

--

- Vito Volterra, Italian, inspired by fisheries data, published 1926

--

*Lotka-Volterra* is used to describe (nearly) any two-species models, generally; but when used alone it refers to their independently-derived predator-prey model

---
# General two-species model

$$\begin{aligned}
\frac{dR}{dt} &= f_1(R, C) \\
\frac{dC}{dt} &= f_2(R, C)
\end{aligned}$$

--

Predators and prey (consumers and resources) also symbolically represented respectively as $V$ and $P$ (in book; victim and predator), $N_1$ and $N_2$, and $H$ and $P$ among others.

---
# **T**he Lotka-Volterra model

We *minimally* have four componenets to add to a model of predator and prey dynamics:

--

1. Prey needs to grow
2. Prey get eaten
3. Predators grow by eating prey
4. Predators must die

--

Let's represent these processes using the following
--

1. $+rR$
--

2. $-aRC$
--

3. $+bRC$
--

4. $-mC$

---
# **T**he Lotka-Volterra model
Putting the pieces together:
1. Prey growth, $+rR$
2. Predators removing prey, $-aRC$
3. Predator growth from eating prey, $+bRC$
4. Predators die, $-mC$
--

$$\begin{aligned}
\frac{dR}{dt} &= rR - aRC \\
\frac{dC}{dt} &= bRC - mC
\end{aligned}$$

--
. . . before we analyze this model, let's consider a simpler, more familiar case between two species

---
# Neutralism
The case where two species do not affect one another.

--

Let's write down the coupled equations for two species, but without interaction, behaving as single species do (logistically). Write each species as $N_1$ and $N_2$.

--

$$\begin{aligned}
\frac{dN_1}{dt} &= r_1N_1 - \alpha _1N_1^2 \\
\frac{dN_2}{dt} &= r_2N_2 - \alpha _2 N_2^2
\end{aligned}$$

or

$$\begin{aligned}
\frac{dN_1}{dt} &= r_1N_1\left(\frac{K_1 - N_1}{K_1}\right) \\
\frac{dN_2}{dt} &= r_2N_2\left(\frac{K_2 - N_2}{K_2}\right)
\end{aligned}$$

---
# Neutralism time series: code
```{r}
neutralism <- function(t, y, parameters) {
  N1 <- y[1]
  N2 <- y[2]
  r1 <- parameters[1]
  r2 <- parameters[2]
  a1 <- parameters[3]
  a2 <- parameters[4]
  dN1 <- r1*N1 - a1*N1^2
  dN2 <- r2*N2 - a2*N2^2
  return(list(c(dN1, dN2)))
}

n.parms <- c(r1 = 0.55, r2 = 0.5, a1 = 0.02, a2 = 0.01)

times <- seq(from = 0, to = 20, by = 0.1)
init <- c(N1 = 2, N2 = 0.5)
neut.out <- ode(y = init, times = times, parms = n.parms, func = neutralism)
```

---
# Neutralism time series: plot
```{r, fig.width = 5, fig.height = 5}
plot(x = neut.out[,1], y= neut.out[,2], lwd = 2, las = 1, xlab = "Time", ylab = "Density", type = "l", ylim = c(0, max(neut.out)), col = "#0000FFFF")
lines(x = neut.out[,1], y= neut.out[,3], lwd = 2, col = "red")
```

---
# Neutralism in state space
```{r, echo = F}
cp <- viridis::viridis(n = length(times), option = "B", alpha = 1/2)
```

```{r, fig.width = 5, fig.height = 5}
plot(x = neut.out[,2], y= neut.out[,3], lwd = 2, las = 1, xlab = bquote(N[1]), ylab = bquote(N[2]), type = "p", ylim = c(0, max(neut.out)), xlim = c(0, max(neut.out)), col = cp, pch = 16)
```

*dark and light colors respectively correspond to early and later points in time*

---
# Neutralism: animated
I. Both $N_1$ and $N_2$ below equilibirum
```{r Neutralism, echo = F, message = FALSE, eval = F}
saveVideo({
  time.seq <- times
  n.frames <- length(time.seq)
  total.time = 15
  ani.options(interval = total.time/n.frames, nmax = n.frames)

  for (i in 1:n.frames) {
  par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 0.1, 0.1), oma = rep(0, 4), mfrow = c(1, 2))
  plot(x = neut.out[1:i,1], y = neut.out[1:i,2], lwd = 2, las = 1, xlab = "Time", ylab = "Density", type = "l", ylim = c(0, max(neut.out)), xlim = c(0, max(neut.out[,1])), col = "#0000FFFF")
  lines(x = neut.out[1:i,1], y= neut.out[1:i,3], lwd = 2, col = "red")
  plot(x = neut.out[1:i,2], y= neut.out[1:i,3], lwd = 2, las = 1, xlab = bquote(N[1]), ylab = bquote(N[2]), type = "l", ylim = c(0, max(neut.out[,2:3])), xlim = c(0, max(neut.out[,2:3])))
  segments(x0 = neut.out[i,2], x1 = neut.out[i,2], y0 = 0, y1 = neut.out[i,3], col = "#0000FFFF", lwd = 1)
  segments(x0 = 0, x1 = neut.out[i,2], y0 = neut.out[i,3], y1 = neut.out[i,3], col = "#FF0000FF", lwd = 1)
  points(x = neut.out[i,2], y = neut.out[i,3], pch = 16)
  }

  }, video.name = "Wk05_PredatorPrey_files/Neutralism.mp4", ani.width = 800, ani.height = 400, ani.dev = function(...){png(res=100,...)}
)
```

<video width="800" height="400" controls loop>
  <source src="Wk05_PredatorPrey_files/Neutralism.mp4" type="video/mp4">
</video>


---
# Neutralism: animated
II. $N_1$ below and $N_2$ above their equilibiria
```{r, echo = F}
init <- c(N1 = 2, N2 = 70)
neut.out.II <- ode(y = init, times = times, parms = n.parms, func = neutralism)

init <- c(N1 = 70, N2 = 0.5)
neut.out.III <- ode(y = init, times = times, parms = n.parms, func = neutralism)

init <- c(N1 = 60, N2 = 70)
neut.out.IV <- ode(y = init, times = times, parms = n.parms, func = neutralism)
```

```{r NeutralismII, echo = F, message = FALSE, eval = F}
saveVideo({
  time.seq <- times
  n.frames <- length(time.seq)
  total.time = 15
  ani.options(interval = total.time/n.frames, nmax = n.frames)

  for (i in 1:n.frames) {
  par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 0.1, 0.1), oma = rep(0, 4), mfrow = c(1, 2))
  plot(x = neut.out.II[1:i,1], y = neut.out.II[1:i,2], lwd = 2, las = 1, xlab = "Time", ylab = "Density", type = "l", ylim = c(0, max(neut.out.II)), xlim = c(0, max(neut.out.II[,1])), col = "#0000FFFF")
  lines(x = neut.out.II[1:i,1], y= neut.out.II[1:i,3], lwd = 2, col = "red")
  plot(x = neut.out.II[1:i,2], y= neut.out.II[1:i,3], lwd = 2, las = 1, xlab = bquote(N[1]), ylab = bquote(N[2]), type = "l", ylim = c(0, max(neut.out.II[,2:3])), xlim = c(0, max(neut.out.II[,2:3])))
  segments(x0 = neut.out.II[i,2], x1 = neut.out.II[i,2], y0 = 0, y1 = neut.out.II[i,3], col = "#0000FFFF", lwd = 1)
  segments(x0 = 0, x1 = neut.out.II[i,2], y0 = neut.out.II[i,3], y1 = neut.out.II[i,3], col = "#FF0000FF", lwd = 1)
  points(x = neut.out.II[i,2], y = neut.out.II[i,3], pch = 16)
  }

  }, video.name = "Wk05_PredatorPrey_files/NeutralismII.mp4", ani.width = 800, ani.height = 400, ani.dev = function(...){png(res=100,...)}
)
```

<video width="800" height="400" controls loop>
  <source src="Wk05_PredatorPrey_files/NeutralismII.mp4" type="video/mp4">
</video>

---
# Neutralism: animated
III. $N_1$ above and $N_2$ below their equilibiria
```{r NeutralismIII, echo = F, message = FALSE, eval = F}
saveVideo({
  time.seq <- times
  n.frames <- length(time.seq)
  total.time = 15
  ani.options(interval = total.time/n.frames, nmax = n.frames)

  for (i in 1:n.frames) {
  par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 0.1, 0.1), oma = rep(0, 4), mfrow = c(1, 2))
  plot(x = neut.out.III[1:i,1], y = neut.out.III[1:i,2], lwd = 2, las = 1, xlab = "Time", ylab = "Density", type = "l", ylim = c(0, max(neut.out.III)), xlim = c(0, max(neut.out.III[,1])), col = "#0000FFFF")
  lines(x = neut.out.III[1:i,1], y= neut.out.III[1:i,3], lwd = 2, col = "red")
  plot(x = neut.out.III[1:i,2], y= neut.out.III[1:i,3], lwd = 2, las = 1, xlab = bquote(N[1]), ylab = bquote(N[2]), type = "l", ylim = c(0, max(neut.out.III[,2:3])), xlim = c(0, max(neut.out.III[,2:3])))
  segments(x0 = neut.out.III[i,2], x1 = neut.out.III[i,2], y0 = 0, y1 = neut.out.III[i,3], col = "#0000FFFF", lwd = 1)
  segments(x0 = 0, x1 = neut.out.III[i,2], y0 = neut.out.III[i,3], y1 = neut.out.III[i,3], col = "#FF0000FF", lwd = 1)
  points(x = neut.out.III[i,2], y = neut.out.III[i,3], pch = 16)
  }

  }, video.name = "Wk05_PredatorPrey_files/NeutralismIII.mp4", ani.width = 800, ani.height = 400, ani.dev = function(...){png(res=100,...)}
)
```

<video width="800" height="400" controls loop>
  <source src="Wk05_PredatorPrey_files/NeutralismIII.mp4" type="video/mp4">
</video>

---
# Neutralism: animated
IV. $N_1$ and $N_2$ above their equilibiria
```{r NeutralismIV, echo = F, message = FALSE, eval = F}
saveVideo({
  time.seq <- times
  n.frames <- length(time.seq)
  total.time = 15
  ani.options(interval = total.time/n.frames, nmax = n.frames)

  for (i in 1:n.frames) {
  par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 0.1, 0.1), oma = rep(0, 4), mfrow = c(1, 2))
  plot(x = neut.out.IV[1:i,1], y = neut.out.IV[1:i,2], lwd = 2, las = 1, xlab = "Time", ylab = "Density", type = "l", ylim = c(0, max(neut.out.IV)), xlim = c(0, max(neut.out.IV[,1])), col = "#0000FFFF")
  lines(x = neut.out.IV[1:i,1], y= neut.out.IV[1:i,3], lwd = 2, col = "red")
  plot(x = neut.out.IV[1:i,2], y= neut.out.IV[1:i,3], lwd = 2, las = 1, xlab = bquote(N[1]), ylab = bquote(N[2]), type = "l", ylim = c(0, max(neut.out.IV[,2:3])), xlim = c(0, max(neut.out.IV[,2:3])))
  segments(x0 = neut.out.IV[i,2], x1 = neut.out.IV[i,2], y0 = 0, y1 = neut.out.IV[i,3], col = "#0000FFFF", lwd = 1)
  segments(x0 = 0, x1 = neut.out.IV[i,2], y0 = neut.out.IV[i,3], y1 = neut.out.IV[i,3], col = "#FF0000FF", lwd = 1)
  points(x = neut.out.IV[i,2], y = neut.out.IV[i,3], pch = 16)
  }

  }, video.name = "Wk05_PredatorPrey_files/NeutralismIV.mp4", ani.width = 800, ani.height = 400, ani.dev = function(...){png(res=100,...)}
)
```

<video width="800" height="400" controls loop>
  <source src="Wk05_PredatorPrey_files/NeutralismIV.mp4" type="video/mp4">
</video>

---
# Neutralism: animated
All four starting points (initial conditions) showing different behaviours
```{r NeutralismALL, echo = F, message = FALSE, eval = F}

saveVideo({
  time.seq <- times
  n.frames <- length(time.seq)
  total.time = 15
  ani.options(interval = total.time/n.frames, nmax = n.frames)

  for (i in 1:n.frames) {
  par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 0.1, 0.1), oma = rep(0, 4), mfrow = c(1, 1))
  plot(x = NA, lwd = 2, las = 1, xlab = bquote(N[1]), ylab = bquote(N[2]), type = "n", ylim = c(0, max(neut.out[,2:3], neut.out.II[,2:3], neut.out.III[,2:3], neut.out.IV[,2:3])), xlim = c(0, max(neut.out[,2:3], neut.out.II[,2:3], neut.out.III[,2:3], neut.out.IV[,2:3])))
  segments(x0 = neut.out[i,2], x1 = neut.out[i,2], y0 = 0, y1 = neut.out[i,3], col = "#0000FFFF", lwd = 1)
  segments(x0 = 0, x1 = neut.out[i,2], y0 = neut.out[i,3], y1 = neut.out[i,3], col = "#FF0000FF", lwd = 1)

  segments(x0 = neut.out.II[i,2], x1 = neut.out.II[i,2], y0 = 0, y1 = neut.out.II[i,3], col = "#0000FFFF", lwd = 1)
  segments(x0 = 0, x1 = neut.out.II[i,2], y0 = neut.out.II[i,3], y1 = neut.out.II[i,3], col = "#FF0000FF", lwd = 1)

  segments(x0 = neut.out.III[i,2], x1 = neut.out.III[i,2], y0 = 0, y1 = neut.out.III[i,3], col = "#0000FFFF", lwd = 1)
  segments(x0 = 0, x1 = neut.out.III[i,2], y0 = neut.out.III[i,3], y1 = neut.out.III[i,3], col = "#FF0000FF", lwd = 1)

  segments(x0 = neut.out.IV[i,2], x1 = neut.out.IV[i,2], y0 = 0, y1 = neut.out.IV[i,3], col = "#0000FFFF", lwd = 1)
  segments(x0 = 0, x1 = neut.out.IV[i,2], y0 = neut.out.IV[i,3], y1 = neut.out.IV[i,3], col = "#FF0000FF", lwd = 1)

  lines(x = neut.out[1:i,2], y= neut.out[1:i,3], lwd = 2)
  lines(x = neut.out.II[1:i,2], y= neut.out.II[1:i,3], lwd = 2)
  lines(x = neut.out.III[1:i,2], y= neut.out.III[1:i,3], lwd = 2)
  lines(x = neut.out.IV[1:i,2], y= neut.out.IV[1:i,3], lwd = 2)

  points(x = neut.out[i,2], y = neut.out[i,3], pch = 16)
  points(x = neut.out.II[i,2], y = neut.out.II[i,3], pch = 16)
  points(x = neut.out.III[i,2], y = neut.out.III[i,3], pch = 16)
  points(x = neut.out.IV[i,2], y = neut.out.IV[i,3], pch = 16)

    }

  }, video.name = "Wk05_PredatorPrey_files/NeutralismALL.mp4", ani.width = 600, ani.height = 600, ani.dev = function(...){png(res=150,...)}
)
```

<video width="400" height="400" controls loop>
  <source src="Wk05_PredatorPrey_files/NeutralismALL.mp4" type="video/mp4">
</video>

---
# Nullclines: the trajectory separators
**Nullclines** describe the curves (including lines) on a phase plane towards or away from which a populations will grow. This is analagous to the concept of an equilibirum, but on a plane (2 dimensions) populations will grow towards or away from a line instead of a point.

--

If we were to plot a logistic-growing population against something arbitrary, like the number of angels dancing on the head of a pin, then we could create a plane that looks something like this:

--

```{r echo = F, fig.width = 4, fig.height = 4}
par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 0.1, 0.1), oma = rep(0, 4))
plot(x = NA, xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1), las = 1, xlab = "Density of N_1", ylab = "Density of Angels on pin head")
```

---
# Nullclines: the trajectory separators
**Nullclines** describe the curves (including lines) on a phase plane towards or away from which a populations will grow. This is analagous to the concept of an equilibirum, but on a plane (2 dimensions) populations will grow towards or away from a line instead of a point.

If we were to plot a logistic-growing population against something arbitrary, like the number of angels dancing on the head of a pin, then we could create a plane that looks something like this:

```{r echo = F, fig.width = 4, fig.height = 4}
par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 0.1, 0.1), oma = rep(0, 4))
plot(x = NA, xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1), las = 1, xlab = "Density of N_1", ylab = "Density of Angels on pin head")
segments(x0 = 2/3, x1 = 2/3, y0 = -1, y1 = 2, lty = 2)
```

---
# Nullclines: the trajectory separators
**Nullclines** describe the curves (including lines) on a phase plane towards or away from which a populations will grow. This is analagous to the concept of an equilibirum, but on a plane (2 dimensions) populations will grow towards or away from a line instead of a point.

If we were to plot a logistic-growing population against something arbitrary, like the number of angels dancing on the head of a pin, then we could create a plane that looks something like this:

```{r echo = F, fig.width = 4, fig.height = 4}
par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 0.1, 0.1), oma = rep(0, 4))
plot(x = NA, xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1), las = 1, xlab = "Density of N_1", ylab = "Density of Angels on pin head")
segments(x0 = 2/3, x1 = 2/3, y0 = -1, y1 = 2, lty = 2)
y.arrows <- seq(from = 0, to = 1, by = 0.1)
n.arrows <- length(y.arrows)
arrows(x0 = rep(0.4, n.arrows), x1 = rep(0.6, n.arrows), y0 = y.arrows, y1 = y.arrows, length = 0.05)
arrows(x1 = rep(0.72, n.arrows), x0 = rep(0.92, n.arrows), y0 = y.arrows, y1 = y.arrows, length = 0.05)
```

---
# Nullclines: the trajectory separators
**Nullclines** describe the curves (including lines) on a phase plane towards or away from which a populations will grow. This is analagous to the concept of an equilibirum, but on a plane (2 dimensions) populations will grow towards or away from a line instead of a point.

If we were to plot a logistic-growing population against something arbitrary, like the number of angels dancing on the head of a pin, then we could create a plane that looks something like this, **or like this**:

```{r echo = F, fig.width = 4, fig.height = 4}
par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 0.1, 0.1), oma = rep(0, 4))
plot(x = NA, xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1), las = 1, ylab = "Density of N_1", xlab = "Density of Angels on pin head")
segments(y0 = 2/3, y1 = 2/3, x0 = -1, x1 = 2, lty = 2)
x.arrows <- seq(from = 0, to = 1, by = 0.1)
n.arrows <- length(x.arrows)
arrows(y0 = rep(0.4, n.arrows), y1 = rep(0.6, n.arrows), x0 = x.arrows, x1 = x.arrows, length = 0.05)
arrows(y1 = rep(0.72, n.arrows), y0 = rep(0.92, n.arrows), x0 = x.arrows, x1 = x.arrows, length = 0.05)
```

---
# Nullclines: the trajectory separators
Now, if we had **two** populations/variables (say, $N_1$ and $N_2$), neither of which are interacting with one another, what would we expect to see?

--

```{r echo = F, fig.width = 4, fig.height = 4, dpi = 100}
par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 0.1, 0.1), oma = rep(0, 4))
plot(x = NA, xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1), las = 1, xlab = "Density of N_1", ylab = "Density of N_2")
segments(y0 = 2/3, y1 = 2/3, x0 = -1, x1 = 2, lty = 2)
segments(x0 = 2/3, x1 = 2/3, y0 = -1, y1 = 2, lty = 2)
x.arrows <- seq(from = 0, to = 1, by = 0.1)
n.arrows <- length(x.arrows)
arrows(y0 = rep(0.4, n.arrows), y1 = rep(0.6, n.arrows), x0 = x.arrows, x1 = x.arrows, length = 0.05, col = "red")
arrows(y1 = rep(0.72, n.arrows), y0 = rep(0.92, n.arrows), x0 = x.arrows, x1 = x.arrows, length = 0.05, col = "red")
y.arrows <- seq(from = 0, to = 1, by = 0.1)
n.arrows <- length(y.arrows)
arrows(x0 = rep(0.4, n.arrows), x1 = rep(0.6, n.arrows), y0 = y.arrows, y1 = y.arrows, length = 0.05, col = "blue")
arrows(x1 = rep(0.72, n.arrows), x0 = rep(0.92, n.arrows), y0 = y.arrows, y1 = y.arrows, length = 0.05, col = "blue")
```

---
# Nullclines: the trajectory separators
Now, if we had **two** populations/variables (say, $N_1$ and $N_2$), neither of which are interacting with one another, what would we expect to see, **more simply**?

```{r echo = F, fig.width = 4, fig.height = 4, dpi = 100}
par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 0.1, 0.1), oma = rep(0, 4))
plot(x = NA, xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1), las = 1, xlab = "Density of N_1", ylab = "Density of N_2")
segments(y0 = 2/3, y1 = 2/3, x0 = -1, x1 = 2, lty = 2)
segments(x0 = 2/3, x1 = 2/3, y0 = -1, y1 = 2, lty = 2)
arrows(x0 = 0.1, y0 = 0.1, x1 = 0.3, y1 = 0.1, length = 0.05, col = "blue", lwd = 2)
arrows(x0 = 0.1, y0 = 0.1, x1 = 0.1, y1 = 0.3, length = 0.05, col = "red", lwd = 2)
arrows(x0 = 0.1, y0 = 1, x1 = 0.3, y1 = 1, length = 0.05, col = "blue", lwd = 2)
arrows(x0 = 0.1, y0 = 1, x1 = 0.1, y1 = 0.8, length = 0.05, col = "red", lwd = 2)
arrows(x0 = 1, y0 = 0.1, x1 = 0.8, y1 = 0.1, length = 0.05, col = "blue", lwd = 2)
arrows(x0 = 1, y0 = 0.1, x1 = 1, y1 = 0.3, length = 0.05, col = "red", lwd = 2)
arrows(x0 = 1, y0 = 1, x1 = 0.8, y1 = 1, length = 0.05, col = "blue", lwd = 2)
arrows(x0 = 1, y0 = 1, x1 = 1, y1 = 0.8, length = 0.05, col = "red", lwd = 2)
```

---
# Nullclines: the trajectory separators
Now, if we had **two** populations/variables (say, $N_1$ and $N_2$), neither of which are interacting with one another, what would we expect to see, **more simply**?

```{r echo = F, fig.width = 4, fig.height = 4, dpi = 100}
par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 0.1, 0.1), oma = rep(0, 4))
plot(x = NA, xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1), las = 1, xlab = "Density of N_1", ylab = "Density of N_2")
segments(y0 = 2/3, y1 = 2/3, x0 = -1, x1 = 2, lty = 2)
segments(x0 = 2/3, x1 = 2/3, y0 = -1, y1 = 2, lty = 2)
arrows(x0 = 0.1, y0 = 0.1, x1 = 0.3, y1 = 0.1, length = 0.05, col = "blue", lwd = 2)
arrows(x0 = 0.1, y0 = 0.1, x1 = 0.1, y1 = 0.3, length = 0.05, col = "red", lwd = 2)
arrows(x0 = 0.1, y0 = 0.1, x1 = 0.3, y1 = 0.3, length = 0.05, col = "black", lwd = 2)

arrows(x0 = 0.1, y0 = 1, x1 = 0.3, y1 = 1, length = 0.05, col = "blue", lwd = 2)
arrows(x0 = 0.1, y0 = 1, x1 = 0.1, y1 = 0.8, length = 0.05, col = "red", lwd = 2)
arrows(x0 = 0.1, y0 = 1, x1 = 0.3, y1 = 0.8, length = 0.05, col = "black", lwd = 2)

arrows(x0 = 1, y0 = 0.1, x1 = 0.8, y1 = 0.1, length = 0.05, col = "blue", lwd = 2)
arrows(x0 = 1, y0 = 0.1, x1 = 1, y1 = 0.3, length = 0.05, col = "red", lwd = 2)
arrows(x0 = 1, y0 = 0.1, x1 = 0.8, y1 = 0.3, length = 0.05, col = "black", lwd = 2)

arrows(x0 = 1, y0 = 1, x1 = 0.8, y1 = 1, length = 0.05, col = "blue", lwd = 2)
arrows(x0 = 1, y0 = 1, x1 = 1, y1 = 0.8, length = 0.05, col = "red", lwd = 2)
arrows(x0 = 1, y0 = 1, x1 = 0.8, y1 = 0.8, length = 0.05, col = "black", lwd = 2)
```

---
# Nullclines: calculation

--

Set $\frac{dN_i}{dt} = 0$ for each equation, put into a graphical form (isolate the variable on the vertical axis), plot, . . . and crush it.

![CRUSH](https://c.tenor.com/s7hjKTx1nJYAAAAC/stranger-things-el.gif)

--

Find the nullclines for the neutralism model . . . and plot them (one for each species).

---
# Nullclines: calculation

The model
$$\begin{aligned}
\frac{dN_1}{dt} &= r_1N_1 - \alpha _1N_1^2 \\
\frac{dN_2}{dt} &= r_2N_2 - \alpha _2N_2^2
\end{aligned}$$

--

Setting each subequation to 0
$$\begin{aligned}
0 &= r_1N_1 - \alpha _1N_1^2 \\
0 &= r_2N_2 - \alpha _2N_2^2
\end{aligned}$$

--

.pull-left[
For $N_1$
$$\begin{aligned}
N^*_1 &= 0 \\
N^*_1 &= \frac{r_1}{\alpha _1}
\end{aligned}$$
]

--

.pull-right[
For $N_2$
$$\begin{aligned}
N^*_2 &= 0 \\
N^*_2 &= \frac{r_2}{\alpha _2}
\end{aligned}$$
]

---
# Nullclines: plot your results
```{r, fig.width = 4, fig.height = 4, echo = F, dpi = 125}
par(mar = c(3, 3, 0.1, 0.1))
plot(x = NA, type = "n", xaxt = "n", yaxt = "n", xlab = "", ylab = "", xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1))
segments(x0 = -1, x1 = 2, y0 = 0, y1 = 0, lwd = 0.75)
segments(y0 = -1, y1 = 2, x0 = 0, x1 = 0, lwd = 0.75)
segments(x0 = -1, x1 = 2, y0 = 0, y1 = 0, lwd = 2, lty = "83", col = "red")
segments(y0 = -1, y1 = 2, x0 = 0, x1 = 0, lwd = 2, lty = "83", col = "blue")
segments(x0 = -1, x1 = 2, y0 = 2/3, y1 = 2/3, lwd = 2, lty = "83", col = "red")
segments(y0 = -1, y1 = 2, x0 = 2/3, x1 = 2/3, lwd = 2, lty = "83", col = "blue")
mtext(side= 1, text = bquote(N[1]), line = 2)
mtext(side= 2, text = bquote(N[2]), line = 2, las = 1)
axis(1, at = c(0, 2/3), labels = c(0, "r_1/a_1"))
axis(2, at = c(0, 2/3), labels = c(0, "r_2/a_2"))
```

---
# Nullclines: plot your results
```{r, fig.width = 4, fig.height = 4, echo = F, dpi = 125}
par(mar = c(3, 3, 0.1, 0.1))
plot(x = NA, type = "n", xaxt = "n", yaxt = "n", xlab = "", ylab = "", xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1))
segments(x0 = -1, x1 = 2, y0 = 0, y1 = 0, lwd = 0.75)
segments(y0 = -1, y1 = 2, x0 = 0, x1 = 0, lwd = 0.75)
segments(x0 = -1, x1 = 2, y0 = 0, y1 = 0, lwd = 2, lty = "83", col = "red")
segments(y0 = -1, y1 = 2, x0 = 0, x1 = 0, lwd = 2, lty = "83", col = "blue")
segments(x0 = -1, x1 = 2, y0 = 2/3, y1 = 2/3, lwd = 2, lty = "83", col = "red")
segments(y0 = -1, y1 = 2, x0 = 2/3, x1 = 2/3, lwd = 2, lty = "83", col = "blue")

mtext(side= 1, text = bquote(N[1]), line = 2)
mtext(side= 2, text = bquote(N[2]), line = 2, las = 1)
axis(1, at = c(0, 2/3), labels = c(0, "r_1/a_1"))
axis(2, at = c(0, 2/3), labels = c(0, "r_2/a_2"))

arrows(x0 = 0.1, y0 = 0.1, x1 = 0.3, y1 = 0.1, length = 0.05, col = "blue", lwd = 2)
arrows(x0 = 0.1, y0 = 0.1, x1 = 0.1, y1 = 0.3, length = 0.05, col = "red", lwd = 2)
arrows(x0 = 0.1, y0 = 0.1, x1 = 0.3, y1 = 0.3, length = 0.05, col = "black", lwd = 2)

arrows(x0 = 0.1, y0 = 1, x1 = 0.3, y1 = 1, length = 0.05, col = "blue", lwd = 2)
arrows(x0 = 0.1, y0 = 1, x1 = 0.1, y1 = 0.8, length = 0.05, col = "red", lwd = 2)
arrows(x0 = 0.1, y0 = 1, x1 = 0.3, y1 = 0.8, length = 0.05, col = "black", lwd = 2)

arrows(x0 = 1, y0 = 0.1, x1 = 0.8, y1 = 0.1, length = 0.05, col = "blue", lwd = 2)
arrows(x0 = 1, y0 = 0.1, x1 = 1, y1 = 0.3, length = 0.05, col = "red", lwd = 2)
arrows(x0 = 1, y0 = 0.1, x1 = 0.8, y1 = 0.3, length = 0.05, col = "black", lwd = 2)

arrows(x0 = 1, y0 = 1, x1 = 0.8, y1 = 1, length = 0.05, col = "blue", lwd = 2)
arrows(x0 = 1, y0 = 1, x1 = 1, y1 = 0.8, length = 0.05, col = "red", lwd = 2)
arrows(x0 = 1, y0 = 1, x1 = 0.8, y1 = 0.8, length = 0.05, col = "black", lwd = 2)
```

---
# Neutralism: using R to graph dynamics
We will use a package called `phaseR` that is found in the **C**omprehensive **R** **A**rchive **N**etwork (CRAN).

--

You can download pacakges using the function `install.packages(pkgs = )`. Let's `install.packages(pkgs = "phaseR")`.

--

Now that it is installed and saved to your personal R library, let's load it: `library(package = "phaseR")`.

---
# Neutralism: using R to graph dynamics

We have to call (1) `parms`, `parameters` **and** (2) `state`, `y` in our function to play with `phaseR` (it's terrible, I know; I am sincerely sorry)

```{r}
neutralism <- function(t, y, parameters) {
  N1 <- y[1]
  N2 <- y[2]
  r1 <- parameters[1]
  r2 <- parameters[2]
  a1 <- parameters[3]
  a2 <- parameters[4]
  dN1 <- r1*N1 - a1*N1^2
  dN2 <- r2*N2 - a2*N2^2
  return(list(c(dN1, dN2)))
}

init <- c(N1 = 0.1, N2 = 0.05)
parm.vals <- c(r1 = 2/3, r2 = 2/3, a1 = 1, a2 = 1)
```
Then, let's plot the nullclines:
```{r message = F, warning = F, eval = F}
nullclines(deriv = neutralism, parameters = parm.vals, xlim = c(-0.1, 1), ylim = c(-0.1, 1), add = F)
```

---
# Neutralism: using R to graph dynamics
```{r echo = F, warning = F, fig.width = 4, fig.height = 4, echo = F, dpi = 125, message = F, results = 'hide'}
par(mar = c(4, 4, 0.1, 0.1))
nullclines(deriv = neutralism, parameters = parm.vals, xlim = c(-0.1, 1), ylim = c(-0.1, 1), add = F, lwd = 2, las = 1, col = c("red", "blue"),
  add.legend = F)
mtext(side = 1, text = bquote(N[1]), line = 3)
mtext(side = 2, text = bquote(N[2]), line = 3, las = 1)
```

---
# Neutralism: using R to graph dynamics
```{r, eval = F}
flowField(deriv = neutralism, xlim = c(-0.1, 1), ylim = c(-0.1, 1), parameters = parm.vals, add = F, points = 20)
```

```{r echo = F, warning = F, fig.width = 4, fig.height = 4, dpi = 100, message = F, results = 'hide'}
par(mar = c(0, 0, 0.1, 0.1), oma = c(4, 4, 0, 0))
flowField(deriv = neutralism, xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1), parameters = parm.vals, add = F, points = 20, las = 1)
mtext(side = 1, text = bquote(N[1]), line = 3)
mtext(side = 2, text = bquote(N[2]), line = 3, las = 1)
```

---
# Neutralism: using R to graph dynamics
Plotting nullclines and direction field together
```{r echo = F, warning = F, fig.width = 4, fig.height = 4, dpi = 125, message = F, results = 'hide'}
par(mar = c(0, 0, 0.1, 0.1), oma = c(4, 4, 0, 0))
flowField(deriv = neutralism, xlim = c(-0.01, 1), ylim = c(-0.01, 1), parameters = parm.vals, y = init, add = F, points = 20, las = 1, xaxs = "i", yaxs = "i")
nullclines(deriv = neutralism, parameters = parm.vals, xlim = c(-0.01, 1), ylim = c(-0.01, 1), add = T, lwd = 2, col = c("red", "blue"), add.legend = F)
mtext(side = 1, text = bquote(N[1]), line = 2.5)
mtext(side = 2, text = bquote(N[2]), line = 2.5, las = 1)
```

---
# Neutralism: using R to graph dynamics
And if you want, you can add trajectories:
```{r, eval = F}
trajectory(deriv = neutralism, y0 = init, t.start = 0, t.end = 25, pch = 16)
```

```{r echo = F, warning = F, fig.width = 4, fig.height = 4, dpi = 100, message = F, results = 'hide'}
par(mar = c(0, 0, 0.1, 0.1), oma = c(4, 4, 0, 0))
flowField(deriv = neutralism, xlim = c(-0.01, 1), ylim = c(-0.01, 1), parameters = parm.vals, y = init, add = F, points = 20, las = 1, xaxs = "i", yaxs = "i")
nullclines(deriv = neutralism, parameters = parm.vals, xlim = c(-0.01, 1), ylim = c(-0.01, 1), add = T, lwd = 2, col = c("red", "blue"), add.legend = F)
trajectory(deriv = neutralism, parameters = parm.vals, y0 = init, tlim = c(0, 25), pch = 16)

mtext(side = 1, text = bquote(N[1]), line = 2.5)
mtext(side = 2, text = bquote(N[2]), line = 2.5, las = 1)
```

---
# A new model: amensalism
**Amensalism**: when one species has a negative effect on another, but that other has no effect on it. A couple biological examples include allelopathy or strong competition.

What might the model look like?

--

The model
$$\begin{aligned}
\frac{dN_1}{dt} &= r_1N_1 - \alpha _1N_1^2 - \beta _1N_1N_2\\
\frac{dN_2}{dt} &= r_2N_2 - \alpha _2N_2^2
\end{aligned}$$

--

Create a phase plane for an amensal relatioship between two species.

---
# Amensalism
The model, initial densities, and parameter values
```{r}
amensalism <- function(t, y, parameters) {
  N1 <- y[1]
  N2 <- y[2]
  r1 <- parameters[1]
  r2 <- parameters[2]
  a1 <- parameters[3]
  a2 <- parameters[4]
  b1 <- parameters[5]
  dN1 <- r1*N1 - a1*N1^2 - b1*N1*N2
  dN2 <- r2*N2 - a2*N2^2
  return(list(c(dN1, dN2)))
}

init <- c(N1 = 0.1, N2 = 0.05)
parm.vals <- c(r1 = 2/3, r2 = 2/3, a1 = 1, a2 = 1, b1 = 0.2)
```

---
# Amensalism
The phase plane

```{r echo = F, warning = F, fig.width = 4, fig.height = 4, dpi = 110, message = F, results = 'hide'}
par(mar = c(0, 0, 0.1, 0.1), oma = c(4, 4, 0, 0))
flowField(deriv = amensalism, xlim = c(-0.01, 1), ylim = c(-0.01, 1), parameters = parm.vals, y = init, add = F, points = 20, las = 1, xaxs = "i", yaxs = "i")
nullclines(deriv = amensalism, parameters = parm.vals, xlim = c(-0.01, 1), ylim = c(-0.01, 1), add = T, lwd = 2, col = c("red", "blue"), add.legend = F)
mtext(side = 1, text = bquote(N[1]), line = 2.5)
mtext(side = 2, text = bquote(N[2]), line = 2.5, las = 1)
```

---
# Amensalism
The phase plane

.pull-left[
```{r echo = F, warning = F, fig.width = 4, fig.height = 4, dpi = 110, message = F, results = 'hide'}
par(mar = c(0, 0, 0.1, 0.1), oma = c(4, 4, 0, 0))
flowField(deriv = amensalism, xlim = c(-0.01, 1), ylim = c(-0.01, 1), parameters = parm.vals, add = F, points = 20, las = 1, xaxs = "i", yaxs = "i")
nullclines(deriv = amensalism, parameters = parm.vals, xlim = c(-0.01, 1), ylim = c(-0.01, 1), add = T, lwd = 2, col = c("red", "blue"), add.legend = F)
mtext(side = 1, text = bquote(N[1]), line = 2.5)
mtext(side = 2, text = bquote(N[2]), line = 2.5, las = 1)
```
]

.pull.right[
Does this make sense?

What are the equations for the nullclines?

]

---
# Amensalism
The model
$$\begin{aligned}
\frac{dN_1}{dt} &= r_1N_1 - \alpha _1N_1^2 - \beta _1N_1N_2\\
\frac{dN_2}{dt} &= r_2N_2 - \alpha _2N_2^2
\end{aligned}$$

--

Setting each subequation to 0
$$\begin{aligned}
0 &= r_1N_1 - \alpha _1N_1^2 - \beta _1N_1N_2 \\
0 &= r_2N_2 - \alpha _2N_2^2
\end{aligned}$$

--

.pull-left[
For $N_1$
$$\begin{aligned}
N^*_1 &= 0 \\
N^*_1 &= \frac{r_1 - \beta _1N_2}{\alpha _1}
\end{aligned}$$
]

--

.pull-right[
For $N_2$
$$\begin{aligned}
N^*_2 &= 0 \\
N^*_2 &= \frac{r_2}{\alpha _2}
\end{aligned}$$
]

---
# Amensalism: varying $\beta$

```{r, echo = F, message = FALSE, eval = F}
beta.seq <- seq(from = 0, to = 1.5, by = 0.01)
beta.parms <- parm.vals

saveVideo({
  n.frames <- length(beta.seq)
  total.time = 15
  ani.options(interval = total.time/n.frames, nmax = n.frames)

  for (i in 1:n.frames) {
    beta.parms[which(names(beta.parms) == "b1")] <- beta.seq[i]
    par(mar = c(0, 0, 1, 1), oma = c(4, 4, 0, 0))
    flowField(deriv = amensalism, xlim = c(-0.01, 1), ylim = c(-0.01, 1), parameters = beta.parms, add = F, points = 20, las = 1, xaxs = "i", yaxs = "i", main = bquote(beta==.(sprintf("%0.2f", beta.seq[i]))), xlab = "", ylab = "")
    nullclines(deriv = amensalism, parameters = beta.parms, xlim = c(-0.01, 1), ylim = c(-0.01, 1), add = T, lwd = 2, col = c("red", "blue"), add.legend = F)
    mtext(side = 1, text = bquote(N[1]), line = 2.5)
    mtext(side = 2, text = bquote(N[2]), line = 2.5, las = 1)

    }
  }, video.name = "Wk05_PredatorPrey_files/Amensalism.mp4", ani.width = 600, ani.height = 600, ani.dev = function(...){png(res=150,...)}
)
```


<video width="500" height="500" controls loop>
  <source src="Wk05_PredatorPrey_files/Amensalism.mp4" type="video/mp4">
</video>


---
# Consumer-resource, both logistic
The model with both species growing logisitcally, but with one species benefiting at the other's expence (say, a consumer-resource interaction)

$$\begin{aligned}
\frac{dN_1}{dt} &= r_1N_1 - \alpha _1N_1^2 - \beta _1N_1N_2\\
\frac{dN_2}{dt} &= r_2N_2 - \alpha _2N_2^2 + \beta_21N_1N_2
\end{aligned}$$

---
# Consumer-resource, both logistic
The model
$$\begin{aligned}
\frac{dN_1}{dt} &= r_1N_1 - \alpha _1N_1^2 - \beta _1N_1N_2\\
\frac{dN_2}{dt} &= r_2N_2 - \alpha _2N_2^2 + \beta_21N_1N_2
\end{aligned}$$

--

Setting each subequation to 0
$$\begin{aligned}
0 &= r_1N_1 - \alpha _1N_1^2 - \beta _1N_1N_2 \\
0 &= r_2N_2 - \alpha _2N_2^2 + \beta_21N_1N_2
\end{aligned}$$

--

.pull-left[
For $N_1$
$$\begin{aligned}
N^*_1 &= 0 \\
N^*_1 &= \frac{r_1 - \beta _1N_2}{\alpha _1}
\end{aligned}$$
]

--

.pull-right[
For $N_2$
$$\begin{aligned}
N^*_2 &= 0 \\
N^*_2 &= \frac{r_2 + \beta _2N_1}{\alpha _2}
\end{aligned}$$
]

---
# Consumer-resource, both logistic
If we were to plot these on a graph, with red lines being the nullclines for $N_1$ and the blue lines being the nullclines for $N_2$, then we'd draw something like this:

```{r, echo = F, fig.width = 6, fig.height = 6}
par(mar = c(4, 4, 0.1, 0.1))
plot(x = NA, xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1), xaxt = "n", yaxt = "n", xlab = "", ylab = "")
mtext(side = 1, text = bquote(N[1]), line = 1)
mtext(side = 2, text = bquote(N[2]), line = 1, las = 1)
axis(side = 1, at = 0, labels = 0)
axis(side = 2, at = 0, labels = 0, las = 1)
segments(x0 = 0, x1 = 0, y0 = 0, y1 = 1, col = "red", lwd = 2)
segments(x0 = 0.5, x1 = 0, y0 = 0, y1 = 0.75, col = "red", lwd = 2)
segments(y0 = 0, y1 = 0, x0 = 0, x1 = 1, col = "blue", lwd = 2)
segments(y0 = 0.25, y1 = 0.75, x0 = 0, x1 = 1, col = "blue", lwd = 2)
```

---
# Consumer-resource, both logistic
The model, initial densities, and parameter values
```{r}
cr.log <- function(t, y, parameters) {
  N1 <- y[1]
  N2 <- y[2]
  r1 <- parameters[1]
  r2 <- parameters[2]
  a1 <- parameters[3]
  a2 <- parameters[4]
  b1 <- parameters[5]
  b2 <- parameters[6]
  dN1 <- r1*N1 - a1*N1^2 - b1*N1*N2
  dN2 <- r2*N2 - a2*N2^2 + b2*N2*N1
  return(list(c(dN1, dN2)))
}

init <- c(N1 = 0.1, N2 = 0.05)
parm.vals <- c(r1 = 2/3, r2 = 2/3, a1 = 1, a2 = 1, b1 = 0.2, b2 = 0.2)
```

---
# Consumer-resource, both logistic
The phase plane

```{r echo = F, warning = F, fig.width = 4, fig.height = 4, dpi = 110, message = F, results = 'hide'}
par(mar = c(0, 0, 0.1, 0.1), oma = c(4, 4, 0, 0))
flowField(deriv = cr.log, xlim = c(-0.01, 1), ylim = c(-0.01, 1), parameters = parm.vals, y = init, add = F, points = 20, las = 1, xaxs = "i", yaxs = "i")
nullclines(deriv = cr.log, parameters = parm.vals, xlim = c(-0.01, 1), ylim = c(-0.01, 1), add = T, lwd = 2, col = c("red", "blue"), add.legend = F)
mtext(side = 1, text = bquote(N[1]), line = 2.5)
mtext(side = 2, text = bquote(N[2]), line = 2.5, las = 1)
```

---
# Consumer-resource, both logistic
```{r CRLog, echo = F, message = FALSE, eval = F}
times <- seq(from = 0, to = 30, by = 0.05)
init <- c(0.1, 0.01)
crlog.out <- ode(y = init, fun = cr.log, parms = parm.vals, times = times)

saveVideo({
  time.seq <- times
  n.frames <- length(time.seq)
  total.time = 15
  ani.options(interval = total.time/n.frames, nmax = n.frames)

  for (i in 1:n.frames) {
  par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 0.2, 0.2), oma = rep(0, 4), mfrow = c(1, 2))
  plot(x = crlog.out[1:i,1], y = crlog.out[1:i,2], lwd = 2, las = 1, xlab = "Time", ylab = "Density", type = "l", ylim = c(0, max(crlog.out[,c(2,3)])), xlim = c(0, max(crlog.out[,1])), col = "#0000FFFF")
  lines(x = crlog.out[1:i,1], y= crlog.out[1:i,3], lwd = 2, col = "red")
flowField(deriv = cr.log, xlim = c(-0.01, 1), ylim = c(-0.01, 1), parameters = parm.vals, y = init, add = F, points = 20, las = 1, xaxs = "i", yaxs = "i", col.lab = "white")
nullclines(deriv = cr.log, parameters = parm.vals, xlim = c(-0.01, 1), ylim = c(-0.01, 1), add = T, lwd = 2, col = c("red", "blue"), add.legend = F)
points(x = crlog.out[i,2], y = crlog.out[i,3], pch = 16)
lines(x = crlog.out[1:i,2], y = crlog.out[1:i,3], lwd = 2)
mtext(side = 1, text = bquote(N[1]), line = 2.5)
mtext(side = 2, text = bquote(N[2]), line = 2.5, las = 1)
  }

  }, video.name = "Wk05_PredatorPrey_files/CRLog.mp4", ani.width = 800, ani.height = 400, ani.dev = function(...){png(res=100,...)}
)
```

<video width="800" height="400" controls loop>
  <source src="Wk05_PredatorPrey_files/CRLog.mp4" type="video/mp4">
</video>

---
# C-R/L-V, R ex. growth and C cons. mort
This is the Lotka-Volterra predator-prey model:

$$\begin{aligned}
\frac{dR}{dt} &= rR - \beta RC\\
\frac{dC}{dt} &= \gamma RC - \delta C
\end{aligned}$$

---
# C-R/L-V, R ex. growth and C cons. mort
The model
$$\begin{aligned}
F_1 = \frac{dR}{dt} &= rR - \beta RC\\
F_2 = \frac{dC}{dt} &= \gamma RC - \delta C
\end{aligned}$$

--

Setting each subequation to 0
$$\begin{aligned}
F_1 = 0 &= rR - \beta RC\\
F_2 = 0 &= \gamma RC - \delta C
\end{aligned}$$

--

.pull-left[
For $F_1$
$$\begin{aligned}
R^* &= 0 \\
C^* &= \frac{r}{\beta}
\end{aligned}$$
]

--

.pull-right[
For $F_2$
$$\begin{aligned}
C^* &= 0 \\
R^* &= \frac{\delta}{\gamma}
\end{aligned}$$
]

---
# C-R/L-V, R ex. growth and C cons. mort
If we were to plot these on a graph, with red lines being the nullclines for $R$ and the blue lines being the nullclines for $C$, then we'd draw something like this:


.pull-left[
```{r, echo = F, fig.width = 4, fig.height = 4}
par(mar = c(4, 4, 0.1, 0.1))
plot(x = NA, xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1), xaxt = "n", yaxt = "n", xlab = "", ylab = "")
mtext(side = 1, text = "R", line = 1)
mtext(side = 2, text = "C", line = 1, las = 1)
axis(side = 1, at = 0, labels = 0)
axis(side = 2, at = 0, labels = 0, las = 1)
segments(x0 = 0, x1 = 0, y0 = 0, y1 = 1, col = "red", lwd = 2)
segments(x0 = 0, x1 = 1, y0 = 0.5, y1 = 0.5, col = "red", lwd = 2)
```
]

--

.pull-right[
```{r, echo = F, fig.width = 4, fig.height = 4}
par(mar = c(4, 4, 0.1, 0.1))
plot(x = NA, xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1), xaxt = "n", yaxt = "n", xlab = "", ylab = "")
mtext(side = 1, text = "R", line = 1)
mtext(side = 2, text = "C", line = 1, las = 1)
axis(side = 1, at = 0, labels = 0)
axis(side = 2, at = 0, labels = 0, las = 1)
segments(y0 = 0, y1 = 0, x0 = 0, x1 = 1, col = "blue", lwd = 2)
segments(y0 = 0, y1 = 1, x0 = 0.5, x1 = 0.5, col = "blue", lwd = 2)
```
]

---
# C-R/L-V, R ex. growth and C cons. mort
The model, initial densities, and parameter values
```{r}
cr.LV <- function(t, y, parameters) {
  R <- y[1]
  C <- y[2]
  r <- parameters[1]
  a <- parameters[2]
  b <- parameters[3]
  m <- parameters[4]
  dR <- r*R - a*R*C
  dC <- b*R*C - m*C
  return(list(c(dR, dC)))
}

init <- c(0.3, 0.5)
parm.vals <- c(r = 0.5, a = 0.5, b = 0.25, m = 0.5)
```

---
# C-R/L-V, R ex. growth and C cons. mort
The phase plane

```{r echo = F, warning = F, fig.width = 4, fig.height = 4, dpi = 110, message = F, results = 'hide'}
par(mar = c(0, 0, 0.1, 0.1), oma = c(4, 4, 0, 0))
flowField(deriv = cr.LV, xlim = c(-0.01, 7), ylim = c(-0.01, 7), parameters = parm.vals, y = init, add = F, points = 20, las = 1, xaxs = "i", yaxs = "i")
nullclines(deriv = cr.LV, parameters = parm.vals, xlim = c(-0.01, 7), ylim = c(-0.01, 7), add = T, lwd = 2, col = c("red", "blue"), add.legend = F)
mtext(side = 1, text = bquote(R), line = 2.5)
mtext(side = 2, text = bquote(C), line = 2.5, las = 1)
```

---
# C-R/L-V, R ex. growth and C cons. mort
```{r CRLV, echo = F, message = FALSE, eval = F}
times <- seq(from = 0, to = 60, by = 0.1)
init <- c(0.3, 0.5)
crLV.out <- ode(y = init, fun = cr.LV, parms = parm.vals, times = times)

saveVideo({
  time.seq <- times
  n.frames <- length(time.seq)
  total.time = 15
  ani.options(interval = total.time/n.frames, nmax = n.frames)

  for (i in 1:n.frames) {
  par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 0.2, 0.2), oma = rep(0, 4), mfrow = c(1, 2), bg = "#006251", col = "#00FFD4FF", col.lab = "#00FFD4FF")
  plot(x = crLV.out[1:i,1], y = crLV.out[1:i,2], lwd = 2, las = 1, xlab = "Time", ylab = "Density", type = "l", ylim = c(0, max(crLV.out[,c(2,3)])), xlim = c(0, max(crLV.out[,1])), col = "#00FFD4FF", axes = F)
  axis(side = 1, col.ticks = "#00FFD4FF", col.axis = "#00FFD4FF")
  axis(side = 2, col.ticks = "#00FFD4FF", col.axis = "#00FFD4FF", las = 1)
  box(col = "#00FFD4FF")
  lines(x = crLV.out[1:i,1], y= crLV.out[1:i,3], lwd = 2, col = "#00FFD4FF", lty = 3)
flowField(deriv = cr.LV, xlim = c(-0.01, 7), ylim = c(-0.01, 7), parameters = parm.vals, y = init, add = F, points = 20, las = 1, xaxs = "i", yaxs = "i", col.lab = "#006251", col = "#00FFD4FF", axes = F)
  axis(side = 1, col.ticks = "#00FFD4FF", col.axis = "#00FFD4FF")
  axis(side = 2,  col.ticks = "#00FFD4FF", col.axis = "#00FFD4FF", las = 1)
  box(col = "#00FFD4FF")
  nullclines(deriv = cr.LV, parameters = parm.vals, xlim = c(-0.01, 7), ylim = c(-0.01, 7), add = T, lwd = 2, col = c("#00FFD4FF", "#00FFD4FF"), add.legend = F, lty = c(1, 3))
points(x = crLV.out[i,2], y = crLV.out[i,3], pch = 16, cex = 1.5)
lines(x = crLV.out[1:i,2], y = crLV.out[1:i,3], lwd = 2)
mtext(side = 1, text = bquote(R), line = 2.5)
mtext(side = 2, text = bquote(C), line = 2.5, las = 1)
  }

  }, video.name = "Wk05_PredatorPrey_files/CRLV_old.mp4", ani.width = 800, ani.height = 400, ani.dev = function(...){png(res=100,...)}
)
```


```{r CRLVold, echo = F, message = FALSE, eval = F}
times <- seq(from = 0, to = 60, by = 0.1)
init <- c(0.3, 0.5)
crLV.out <- ode(y = init, fun = cr.LV, parms = parm.vals, times = times)

saveVideo({
  time.seq <- times
  n.frames <- length(time.seq)
  total.time = 15
  ani.options(interval = total.time/n.frames, nmax = n.frames)

  for (i in 1:n.frames) {
  par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 0.2, 0.2), oma = rep(0, 4), mfrow = c(1, 2))
  plot(x = crLV.out[1:i,1], y = crLV.out[1:i,2], lwd = 2, las = 1, xlab = "Time", ylab = "Density", type = "l", ylim = c(0, max(crLV.out[,c(2,3)])), xlim = c(0, max(crLV.out[,1])), col = "blue")
  lines(x = crLV.out[1:i,1], y= crLV.out[1:i,3], lwd = 2, col = "red")
flowField(deriv = cr.LV, xlim = c(-0.01, 7), ylim = c(-0.01, 7), parameters = parm.vals, y = init, add = F, points = 20, las = 1, xaxs = "i", yaxs = "i", col.lab = "white")
  nullclines(deriv = cr.LV, parameters = parm.vals, xlim = c(-0.01, 7), ylim = c(-0.01, 7), add = T, lwd = 2, col = c("blue", "red"), add.legend = F)
points(x = crLV.out[i,2], y = crLV.out[i,3], pch = 16, cex = 1.5)
lines(x = crLV.out[1:i,2], y = crLV.out[1:i,3], lwd = 2)
mtext(side = 1, text = bquote(R), line = 2.5)
mtext(side = 2, text = bquote(C), line = 2.5, las = 1)
  }

  }, video.name = "Wk05_PredatorPrey_files/CRLV.mp4", ani.width = 800, ani.height = 400, ani.dev = function(...){png(res=100,...)}
)
```

<video width="800" height="400" controls loop>
  <source src="Wk05_PredatorPrey_files/CRLV_old.mp4" type="video/mp4">
</video>

---
class: inverse, center
background-image: url(http://www2.nau.edu/lrm22/lessons/predator_prey/hare_lynx_graph.png)
background-size: contain

# Snowshoe hare-lynx data from the Hudson Bay Trapping Company

---
# C-R, R log. growth and C cons. mort.
This is the Lotka-Volterra predator-prey model with generalist prey:

$$\begin{aligned}
\frac{dR}{dt} &= rR - \alpha R^2 - \beta RC\\
\frac{dC}{dt} &= \gamma RC - \delta C
\end{aligned}$$

---
# C-R, R log. growth and C cons. mort.
The model
$$\begin{aligned}
F_1 = \frac{dR}{dt} &= rR - \alpha R^2 - \beta RC\\
F_2 = \frac{dC}{dt} &= \gamma RC - \delta C
\end{aligned}$$

--

Setting each subequation to 0
$$\begin{aligned}
F_1 = 0 &= rR - \alpha R^2 - \beta RC\\
F_2 = 0 &= \gamma RC - \delta C
\end{aligned}$$

--

.pull-left[
For $F_1$
$$\begin{aligned}
R &= 0 \\
R &= \frac{r - \beta C}{\alpha}
\end{aligned}$$
]

--

.pull-right[
For $F_2$
$$\begin{aligned}
C &= 0 \\
R &= \frac{\delta}{\gamma}
\end{aligned}$$
]


---
# C-R, R log. growth and C cons. mort.
The model, initial densities, and parameter values
```{r}
cr.LVDD <- function(t, y, parameters) {
  R <- y[1]
  C <- y[2]
  r <- parameters[1]
  alpha <- parameters[2]
  a <- parameters[3]
  b <- parameters[4]
  m <- parameters[5]
  dR <- r*R - alpha*R*R - a*R*C
  dC <- b*R*C - m*C
  return(list(c(dR, dC)))
}

init <- c(0.3, 0.5)
parm.vals <- c(r = 0.5, alpha = 0.2, a = 0.25, b = 0.25, m = 0.4)
```

---
# C-R, R log. growth and C cons. mort.
The phase plane

```{r echo = F, warning = F, fig.width = 4, fig.height = 4, dpi = 110, message = F, results = 'hide'}
par(mar = c(0, 0, 0.1, 0.1), oma = c(4, 4, 0, 0))
a <- flowField(deriv = cr.LVDD, xlim = c(-0.01, 3), ylim = c(-0.01, 3), parameters = parm.vals, y = init, add = F, points = 20, las = 1, xaxs = "i", yaxs = "i")
a <- nullclines(deriv = cr.LVDD, parameters = parm.vals, xlim = c(-0.01, 3), ylim = c(-0.01, 3), add = T, lwd = 2, col = c("red", "blue"), add.legend = F)
mtext(side = 1, text = bquote(R), line = 2.5)
mtext(side = 2, text = bquote(C), line = 2.5, las = 1)
```

---
# C-R, R log. growth and C cons. mort.
```{r CRLVDD, echo = F, message = FALSE, eval = F}
times <- seq(from = 0, to = 30, by = 0.1)
init <- c(0.3, 0.5)
crLVDD.out <- ode(y = init, fun = cr.LVDD, parms = parm.vals, times = times)

saveVideo({
  time.seq <- times
  n.frames <- length(time.seq)
  total.time = 15
  ani.options(interval = total.time/n.frames, nmax = n.frames)

  for (i in 1:n.frames) {
  par(mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 0.2, 0.2), oma = rep(0, 4), mfrow = c(1, 2))
  plot(x = crLVDD.out[1:i,1], y = crLVDD.out[1:i,2], lwd = 2, las = 1, xlab = "Time", ylab = "Density", type = "l", ylim = c(0, max(crLVDD.out[,c(2,3)])), xlim = c(0, max(crLVDD.out[,1])), col = "#0000FFFF")
  lines(x = crLVDD.out[1:i,1], y= crLVDD.out[1:i,3], lwd = 2, col = "red")
a <- flowField(deriv = cr.LVDD, xlim = c(-0.01, 3), ylim = c(-0.01, 3), parameters = parm.vals, y = init, add = F, points = 20, las = 1, xaxs = "i", yaxs = "i", col.lab = "white")
a <- nullclines(deriv = cr.LVDD, parameters = parm.vals, xlim = c(-0.01, 3), ylim = c(-0.01, 3), add = T, lwd = 2, col = c("red", "blue"), add.legend = F)
points(x = crLVDD.out[i,2], y = crLVDD.out[i,3], pch = 16, cex = 1.5)
lines(x = crLVDD.out[1:i,2], y = crLVDD.out[1:i,3], lwd = 2)
mtext(side = 1, text = bquote(R), line = 2.5)
mtext(side = 2, text = bquote(C), line = 2.5, las = 1)
  }

  }, video.name = "Wk05_PredatorPrey_files/CRLVDD.mp4", ani.width = 800, ani.height = 400, ani.dev = function(...){png(res=100,...)}
)
```

<video width="800" height="400" controls loop>
  <source src="Wk05_PredatorPrey_files/CRLVDD.mp4" type="video/mp4">
</video>
